# Preview-language exploration: the Signal-glow candidate behind a previewLanguage switch

## Context

The G5 lane of the multi-window docking choreography planning Discussion (D#15204): the wow tier needs a chosen **preview design language**, and OQ4's design-authority draft (discussion comment `DC_kwDODSospM4BDW6g`) specifies three complete candidates — «Blueprint», «Signal glow», «Solid press» — with a selection protocol: build the lead candidate live behind a switch, compare side-by-side on the same drag, operator picks on sight, losing candidates' organs transplant.

This leaf builds the lead: **Candidate 2, «Signal glow»** — the mission-control identity (`--fm-signal` flood + condensed boundary ring on the target zone; §06 chips re-skinned dark with teal iconography and an opacity-only hover-lock pulse; a teal edge-light on the drag proxy that the popup vessel's chrome will later share; commit = the flood condenses to the pane's entering edge and extinguishes over `--motion-panel`/`--ease-out-soft`).

## Ungraduated-Discussion exception (creation-gate declaration)

D#15204 is high-blast and not yet graduated; this leaf passes the block on the substantive-rationale path:
1. **Rationale + attribution:** the Discussion body's G5 lane is author-claimed (Clio, design authority) and survived cycle-1 peer divergence unchallenged; the OQ4 comment is the build-ready spec; the operator's product direction (2026-07-16 morning) named the design exploration explicitly. This leaf is **low-blast** (bounded SCSS/skin variants + one config switch, single PR, no cross-substrate coupling) — exploration content, not graduation pre-emption.
2. **Divergence substance inline:** the three candidates in the OQ4 comment ARE the divergence matrix for this design space (each with thesis + risk + falsifier-by-comparison); this leaf builds ONE for live evaluation, it does not adopt it as the ruling.
3. **Downstream-amendment acknowledgment:** on D#15204 graduation this leaf re-parents under the minted epic; the operator's selection may retire the variant, transplant organs from the other candidates, or demand a second candidate build — the switch architecture makes all three cheap.

## The Problem

The shipped drop-indicator/preview family (`resources/scss/src/dashboard/Container.scss` §186–260, the `#14974` §06 menu + the workstation `DockPreview` renderer) is functional but visually neutral — it proves mechanics, not the wow bar. The release recording needs a treatment with an identity, chosen by eye against live alternatives, not asserted in prose.

## The Architectural Reality

- The indicator visual family is token-fed and motion-contract-clean already (`--dock-transition-*`); the vocabulary beneath it lands in PR #15205 (`--motion-*`/`--ease-*`, all five themes). The skin variants consume BOTH tiers, zero literals.
- The switch is a host-level modifier cls (`neo-preview-lang-signal`) on the dock host — same mechanism as the theme cls, no JS behavior change; the workstation exposes it as a `previewLanguage` config (app tier) so tours and the operator can flip it live.
- The §06 candidate grammar, hit-testing, and commit semantics are UNTOUCHED — this is skin, not behavior (the binding constraint from the OQ4 draft).
- Light mode consumes the deep-pigment teal (`--fm-signal: #0f766e`) from the #14681 landing; dark keeps the glow teal — one identity, two renditions.

## The Fix

One PR: (1) the `.neo-preview-lang-signal` variant blocks for the indicator cross + edge chips + zone-preview flood in the dashboard skin (and the workstation `DockPreview` skin where the flood renders); (2) the workstation `previewLanguage` config threading the modifier cls; (3) a before/after recording pair (default vs signal) on the same scripted drag; (4) both modes verified with computed-style receipts.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.neo-preview-lang-signal` renders the full Signal-glow treatment (flood ≤22% mix, boundary ring, chip re-skin, hover-lock pulse opacity-only, proxy edge-light) with ZERO duration/easing/color literals — vocabulary + palette tokens only (review-checkable).
- [ ] Default (unswitched) rendering is byte-identical to today — the variant is strictly additive.
- [ ] The workstation `previewLanguage` config flips the language live; the tour can script the flip.
- [ ] Both color modes verified (computed receipts); reduced-motion collapses through the token layer with nothing to override.
- [ ] Before/after recording pair in the PR body (the render-verify precedent).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope

Building «Blueprint»/«Solid press» (organs transplant AFTER the operator's pick) · any §06 grammar/behavior change · the popup-vessel chrome treatment (rides the G1 adapter lane — the edge-light spec is recorded in OQ4 for it) · the selection decision itself (operator's eye, on the Discussion).

## Related

Parent #13158 · D#15204 (G5 lane; re-parents on graduation) · consumes PR #15205 (motion vocabulary) + the #14681 palette · skins #14974's §06 layer · the workstation app (#15099 arc).

Live latest-open sweep: latest 10 at 2026-07-16T06:27Z; "preview language"/"signal glow" searches return no equivalent (nearest #14930 = a different, closed polish pass). A2A in-flight: [lane-claim] broadcast MESSAGE:af9ef0fe sent this minute; G5 unchallenged through D#15204 cycle-1.

- 2026-07-16T06:40:14Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `3a93d2c` - "feat(workstation): previewLanguage config — the live design-language switch (#15206)

Second slice of the G5 exploration: the workstation Workspace gains a reactive
previewLanguage config that maps its value to a neo-preview-lang-<value>
modifier cls on the dock host — the selection-protocol mechanism: skin variants
swap live from the workspace config, a tour script, or the console, without
touching behavior. Null renders the default affordance family.

The afterSet fires before the dock host exists during construction, so the
construct-time add path re-applies the active value once the host is live —
both orders converge on the same cls state (guarded via getReference).

Verified live end-to-end (dev server + Neural Link): worker-side
set({previewLanguage: 'signal'}) on the running Workspace instance landed
neo-preview-lang-signal on the .workstation-dock-host element in the real DOM
alongside the untouched base cls set.

Remaining on this lane: the zone-preview flood treatment + the before/after
recording pair, then the PR.

## Author AC addendum — this leaf carries `#14780`'s AC-4 (normative; joins the body's AC list at the next body-sync)

- [ ] **AC-7 (carried from the motion-standards ticket, per PR #15205 cycle-1 review):** the review-gate checklist line — *"a hard cut or layout-thrashing animation on a demo/product surface = Required Action"* — lands in the reviewer-facing demo-surface checklist as part of this PR. Exact placement (the pr-review template's audit family vs a demo-conformance reference) is settled in-PR with the cross-family reviewer; this ticket is the **durable owner** (the prior carrier closed without it; a reopen-trigger is not ownership). If this leaf closes without delivering it, the AC reverts to the motion-standards ticket and blocks any successor close.

- 2026-07-16T06:42:31Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #15205
- 2026-07-16T06:45:38Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by #15207
- 2026-07-16T06:49:04Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `7aa118f` - "feat(agentos): Signal-glow zone-preview flood — condensed ring + theme-wide identity (#15206)

Third slice of the G5 exploration: under the .neo-preview-lang-signal host
modifier, the zone-preview accept language binds to the FM signal on EVERY
theme (deep-pigment teal on light skins, glow teal on dark — the dark skin's
domain aliases already map accept to the signal column; this variant makes the
identity theme-universal instead of dark-only) and the accepted zone gains a
condensed 1px inset boundary ring plus a soft outer bloom. Insertion guides
(split / tab-before / tab-after) carry the signal bar with a tightened glow.
The rejected language is deliberately untouched — rejection stays unmistakably
itself. Strictly additive; the default family renders unchanged without the
modifier.

Verified: compiled CSS carries the variant selectors; live computed receipt on
the dev server (probe under the flipped host): background signal @ 18% alpha,
border rgb(94,234,212), inset-ring + bloom shadow stack.

Remaining on this lane: the Demo-A side-by-side recording pair, then the PR
(carrying the inherited review-gate checklist AC).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T06:52:18Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #15208
- 2026-07-16T06:52:38Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `0787a3f` - "feat(dashboard): Signal-glow preview-language variant — indicator/chip skin slice (#15206)

First slice of the G5 design exploration (the D-15204 OQ4 lead candidate): the
.neo-preview-lang-signal host modifier re-skins the drop-indicator/chip family
to the mission-control identity — signal-teal glyphs/borders on deep ground,
signal-mixed active fill + glow shadow, and an opacity-only hover-lock breath
(replays per re-lock via the class toggle). Strictly additive: without the
modifier the default family renders unchanged; the section-06 grammar,
hit-testing, and commit semantics are untouched — skin, never behavior.

Durations/easings stay token-only through the dock aliases (reduced-motion
collapses with nothing to override); color fallbacks mirror the dark-skin token
values, the family's shipped pattern.

Verified live (dev server, probe element on the dock example): variant applies
under the modifier — border rgb(94,234,212) [--fm-signal], signal-mix ground,
animation neo-preview-signal-lock @ 0.12s via --dock-transition-duration-fast,
teal-mixed glow. Compiled CSS carries 9 variant selector hits; default block
byte-untouched. Observation for the side-by-side recording: this example's
default accept accent already reads teal-adjacent — the visible delta is
ground/glow/pulse, which the recording increment must frame accordingly.

Next increments on this lane: the workstation previewLanguage config switch +
zone-preview flood treatment + the before/after recording pair.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T06:55:43Z @neo-fable-clio added this to the **v13.2** milestone
- 2026-07-16T06:58:43Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by #13158
- 2026-07-16T07:20:15Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `1200a0e` - "fix(theme): Signal-glow rides the domain-alias boundary — app-projected, census-true (#15206)

Cycle-1 review (Emmy) convicted the architectural seam: the variant consumed
--fm-* directly in SHARED SCSS with dark fallbacks — but the workstation loads
--workstation-*, not the AgentOS FM palette, so the 'theme-universal' claim was
fallback-masked on the flagship itself (the exact bug class from the motion-
vocabulary landing), and the declared DockPreview consumer census was red.

The repair extends the settled component-domain alias surface instead:

- Shared SCSS (dashboard Container + structural DockPreview) now consumes ONLY
  the --agent-dock-preview-signal(-fill/-ground) aliases — zero --fm-* remains;
  the census passes unchanged and gains signal-trio both-skin assertions.
- The AgentOS theme DockPreview files define the signal trio per mode (dark
  glow column / daylight deep pigment + paper-safe fill + near-white chip
  ground — light chips float, they don't glow).
- The Workstation theme files project ALL SEVEN aliases from ITS OWN palette —
  including accept/reject, which the flagship had silently never projected
  (its default preview language ran on fallbacks before this commit).

Verified: census spec 37/37 green (red at the reviewed head); live token-
presence receipts on the workstation BOTH modes — dark: signal #5eead4 via
--workstation-signal; light (app toggle): signal #0f766e, paper fill, white
chip ground — values unreachable at the reviewed head.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T07:24:53Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `1e19981` - "feat(dashboard): drag-proxy edge-light + previewLanguage contract pins (#15206)

AC-1's missing surface: the drag-proxy edge-light. The proxy mounts at
document.body (DragZone's proxyParentId default), outside any dock host, so the
host modifier cannot scope it by descent — :root:has(.neo-preview-lang-signal)
lifts the scope to the page: any document carrying a signal-flipped host lights
its proxies with the alias-driven ring + bloom. One language in-window and out;
the popup-vessel chrome of the multi-window arc inherits this exact treatment.

Workspace.spec.mjs gains the previewLanguage contract pins (the review's named
location): initial-value convergence (afterSet fires pre-host; the construct
re-apply converges both orders), live swap (old modifier leaves, new lands),
null reset (no modifier remains), and the open-selector rule (any candidate
name maps; unknown values are inert cls tokens, never errors).

Verified: workstation unit suite 3/3 green; themes rebuild clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T07:30:17Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `8e0512c` - "test(visual): the preview-language specimen pair — regression-guarded goldens, both modes (#15206)

AC-5's pair lands as visual-regression baselines (the design-gate harness
pattern): one deterministic specimen board — DEFAULT vs SIGNAL GLOW, the real
affordance classes (indicator chip, hover-locked chip, accepted zone, accepted
split bar) side by side — captured per color mode, scoped to the board element
so the living workstation never affects the goldens.

Three determinism repairs learned while minting (each a fallback-masking
cousin): the DockPreview skin is injected until the affordance-layers leaf
merges (this branch's workstation loads no preview CSS); the light capture
settles on a computed-palette SIGNAL (--workstation-ground === the daylight
value), never a sleep; and the board mounts INSIDE the themed subtree — the
workstation's theme class rides an inner root, so a body-mounted board
resolved dark fallbacks in both modes.

The light golden shows the projected daylight language: paper ground,
deep-pigment #0f766e floods and bars, signal chips on near-white grounds —
values unreachable before the domain-alias repair.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T07:32:55Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `a1ee10a` - "feat(skills): the demo-surface motion audit — AC-7's reviewer gate lands (#15206)

The motion-standards ticket's review-gate line becomes reviewer-facing
substrate, wired exactly like the sibling audits: one compress-to-trigger line
in pr-review-guide.md (7.5.3) + the audit atlas loaded only when a diff touches
demo/product-surface rendered motion.

The audit carries the ratified rule set verbatim: hard-cut-or-layout-thrash =
Required Action (with the two accepted corrections: instant paths must be
intentional and NAMED; transform/opacity are the default palette, other
properties need a bounded named reason + motion evidence), token-only timing,
and the motion-evidence expectation.

Slot rationale (turn-memory-pre-flight decision tree): lifecycle-event
substrate -> skill-family atlas, not AGENTS.md; loaded-bytes delta = one guide
line (the atlas loads on trigger only). Retirement trigger named in-file: a
mechanical motion lint supersedes the reviewer discipline.

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-16T07:34:06Z

**Superseded (cycle-2 RA-5):** the normative Contract Ledger now lives in the ticket body above, truthed to the repaired head — this comment held the cycle-1 backfill and is retained only as history.

— Clio (@neo-fable-clio · Fable 5), leaf author 📜

- 2026-07-16T08:45:42Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `306adaa` - "fix(dashboard): the dock proxy carries its scope — ownership, language, nearest-ancestor theme (#15206)

The cycle-2 falsifier proved the drag-proxy edge-light escaped both the docking owner
(generic .neo-dragproxy capture) and the theme subtree (body-mounted dark fallback in
light mode). The embodiment now CARRIES its scope instead:

- DockTabSortZone#getDragProxyConfig stamps the dock-ownership marker, the host's
  neo-preview-lang-* modifier, and the NEAREST-ancestor theme (one parent walk;
  getTheme() only as the no-ancestor fallback — it resolves the outer boot theme,
  wrong for apps that theme-swap an inner root while body keeps the boot theme).
- DragZone#createDragProxy yields to an explicit theme in the proxy config: pushing
  the derived boot theme next to a carried theme left the winner to stylesheet order.
- Shared signal SCSS drops every literal fallback (an equal-valued literal masks a
  missing projection in exactly one mode) and scopes the edge-light to
  .neo-dock-dragproxy.neo-preview-lang-signal — no :root:has(), no generic capture.
- Census: signal no-fallback + ownership-selector pins + the workstation seven-alias
  both-mode projection census; new DockTabSortZone contract pins including the
  nearest-ancestor-beats-boot-theme case.

Live receipts (real drags, workstation dev): dark proxy = projected #5eead4, light
proxy = projected #0f766e with exactly one carried theme cls — the falsified scene
inverted at the seam it failed on.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T08:45:42Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `b89d92d` - "test(e2e): the same-real-drag capture pair — default/signal, both modes, reduced-motion (#15206)

AC-5 as executable evidence: ONE committed pointer gesture on Demo-A (whitebox Neural
Link harness), parked deterministically over the editor zone, captured under DEFAULT
and SIGNAL in BOTH color modes — target zone flood, the five-position indicator cross,
and the body-mounted drag proxy in every frame. Four regression-guarded goldens.

In-spec truths beyond the pixels: the language modifier never rides a default-gesture
proxy; the signal proxy carries exactly ONE theme cls (nearest-ancestor, light #0f766e
— the cycle-2 falsifier scene on a live gesture); under prefers-reduced-motion the
signal path's every animation/transition entry computes 0s through the vocabulary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T08:45:42Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `84e5fa0` - "docs(skills): motion-audit evidence honesty + full retirement coverage (#15206)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T09:09:35Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #15237
